content.content:
Web search results for query: "CSS masonry specification "grid integrated" "grid independent" proposals 2024 2025"

Links: [{"title":"CSS Masonry & CSS Grid | CSS-Tricks","url":"https://css-tricks.com/css-masonry-css-grid/"},{"title":"Masonry In CSS: Should Grid Evolve Or Stand Aside For A New Module? — Smashing Magazine","url":"https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/05/masonry-css-should-grid-evolve-stand-aside-new-module/"},{"title":"CSS WG Blog – First Public Working Draft of CSS Grid Level 3 / Masonry Layout","url":"https://www.w3.org/blog/CSS/2024/09/24/css-grid-3-fpwd/"},{"title":"Help us choose the final syntax for Masonry in CSS | WebKit","url":"https://webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/"},{"title":"Brick by brick: Help us build CSS Masonry | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry-update"},{"title":"Choosing a Masonry Syntax in CSS | OddBird","url":"https://www.oddbird.net/2024/10/01/grid-masonry-nuance/"},{"title":"CSS WG Blog – CSS Grid: Level 1 & 2 updated, Level 3 (masonry layout) reworked","url":"https://www.w3.org/blog/CSS/2025/03/28/css-grid-1-2-3-updated/"},{"title":"Feedback needed: How should we define CSS masonry? | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry-syntax"},{"title":"An alternative proposal for CSS masonry | Blog | Chrome for Developers","url":"https://developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry"},{"title":"[css-grid-3][masonry] Discussion overview for masonry syntax discussions · Issue #11593 · w3c/csswg-drafts","url":"https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/11593"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about CSS masonry specification proposals in 2024-2025:

## Overview

The CSS Working Group published a First Public Working Draft of CSS Grid Layout Module Level 3 (CSS Masonry Layout) in September 2024, representing masonry layout with two possible syntactic forms: a grid-integrated syntax and a grid-independent syntax.

## The Two Main Proposals

**Grid-Integrated Syntax**: In this syntax, CSS masonry is directly integrated into CSS grid, with the masonry mode triggered by applying the keyword masonry to the grid-template-columns or grid-template-rows definition.

**Grid-Independent Syntax**: This syntax introduces CSS masonry as its own display type. A display value named masonry kicks off a flow-based system where items stack vertically by default, adjusting horizontally to fit the container, controlled with properties like masonry-direction and gap.

## Consensus and Evolution

The CSSWG resolved to adopt fully mixed track sizing for masonry layout, allowing all track listings expressible in CSS Grid for masonry as well, which unifies the two proposals' underlying layout models so both syntactic forms represent essentially equal capabilities.

The March 2025 update to Level 3 resolved key debates in masonry layout and incorporates the CSSWG's resolution to re-use grid-* properties for track templating and item placement.

## Additional Third Proposal

In March 2025, Apple's WebKit team proposed Item Flow, a new system that unifies concepts from Flexbox, Grid, and masonry into a single set of properties, replacing flex-flow and grid-auto-flow with a shorthand called item-flow.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
